inline_comments.message: Is your intention to return an immutable list? I would use Collections.unmodifiableList instead. Wasn't clear if the constructor actually creates new copies of the elements. If that matters.

Yes, this will create a different list from the class one, part of this base class.   The way I phrased it was a bit faulty, what I meant that any additions/removals to the list by the caller won't have any effects in the master list, won't have any effects here on the list membership (just on the list membership).   I think it is ok.
